mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS rate transmits through a cheaper discount rate and easier financial conditions, lifting equity multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is still easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window, or if the signal's rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in CPIAUCSL above the Fed's comfort zone raises expected policy tightening and real-rate pressure, which compresses valuation multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend rises above 2.5% annualized or FEDFUNDS is below CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation by more than 1 pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the forward return distribution is not significantly below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and consumption momentum, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when unemployment is rising from already-low levels.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average return after the trigger is not negative versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE indicates broad labor-market deterioration, which is a stronger recession warning than either series alone and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up condition does not outperform either single series in explaining 20-day SPX weakness, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 45% for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales growth in RSAFS signals softer household demand and slower revenue growth, which depresses near-term equity earnings revisions and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth falls below 0% on a year-over-year basis
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS growth does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional average 5-day return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and real activity, which supports cyclical earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO trend periods fail to generate above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST indicates housing-led demand recovery that feeds through construction, durable goods, and wealth effects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% versus its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout episodes do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the mean conditional return is not above unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is positive and payroll growth is improving at the same time, the economy is in broad-based expansion, and that joint signal should forecast positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter better than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1/PAYEMS signal does not outperform single-series models in walk-forward tests, or if positive 60-day SPX returns do not occur in at least 55% of synchronized expansion instances.
